name: wherever_no_writes_external_body
code:
proof fn phi_1_wherever_no_writes_external_body(
    pm_region_view: PersistentMemoryRegionView,
    s: Seq<u8>,
    addr: int,
)
    requires
        pm_region_view.can_crash_as(s),
        0 <= addr < s.len(),
        pm_region_view.state[addr].outstanding_write.is_none(),
    ensures
        s[addr] == pm_region_view.committed()[addr],
{
    lemma_wherever_no_outstanding_writes_persistent_memory_view_can_only_crash_as_committed(pm_region_view);
}
reason: `lemma_wherever_no_outstanding_writes_persistent_memory_view_can_only_crash_as_committed` is `external_body` — the per-address crash determinism for bytes without outstanding writes is trusted without proof; if a chunk with mixed outstanding/non-outstanding bytes could crash as flushed, the non-outstanding byte would change

name: crash_as_preserves_length
code:
proof fn phi_2_crash_as_preserves_length(view: PersistentMemoryRegionView, s: Seq<u8>)
    requires
        view.can_crash_as(s),
    ensures
        s.len() == view.len(),
{
}
reason: Any valid crash state must have the same length as the region — if crash states could have different lengths, all index-based reasoning would be invalid

name: chunk_vacuous_for_out_of_bounds
code:
proof fn phi_3_chunk_vacuous_for_out_of_bounds(view: PersistentMemoryRegionView, bytes: Seq<u8>)
    requires
        view.len() > 0,
    ensures
        view.chunk_corresponds_ignoring_outstanding_writes(-1, bytes),
        view.chunk_corresponds_after_flush(-1, bytes),
{
}
reason: Negative chunk indices are vacuously true because no valid address divides to a negative chunk — if the chunk predicate weren't vacuous for invalid chunks, `can_crash_as` would impose impossible constraints

name: committed_at_addr_equals_state_at_last_flush
code:
proof fn phi_4_committed_at_addr_equals_state_at_last_flush(view: PersistentMemoryRegionView, addr: int)
    requires
        0 <= addr < view.len(),
    ensures
        view.committed()[addr] == view.state[addr].state_at_last_flush,
{
}
reason: `committed()` should project `state_at_last_flush` for each byte — if the map produced different values, crash recovery would read corrupted data

name: outstanding_write_in_chunk_allows_flushed_crash
reason: A full chunk with outstanding writes should be able to crash as the flushed state — tests that the crash model allows the flushed branch per chunk; if it didn't, pending writes could never become visible after a crash
